Petition Of Jean Ghiglieri For (1) An Order Confirming The Share Of Each Beneficiary In The Trust, (2) An Order Determining The Fair Market Value Of The Trust Property, And (3) An Order Confirming That Petitioner Has The Option To Purchase At That Value, With A Reasonable Time To Arrange Financing
Off Calendar Feb-24-2014 Continued To Apr-07-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Feb-10-2014 Continued To Mar-17-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara De Vries (Trustee). Michael Melchin for and with Rose Curreri. Alvin Buchignani for and with Jean Ghiglieri.

Mr. Siracusa stated that the Trustee feels that she should not take a position because she has a duty of neutrality to all Beneficiaries, and that his Client tried to assist with resolution but that it never happened. The Court stated that there is no petition before the Court asking for a specific relief and that instead there is a petition stating that there is a mess and asking the Court to sort it out. Mr. Melchin and Mr. Buchignani asked the Court to proceed and stated that they did not feel mediation would be helpful. Mr. Buchignani also raised the issue of commissions and capital gains tax if the property were to be marketed. Mr. Siracusa also stated that they set forth in the petition what the various options are and urged the Court to ask the Parties about their positions and proceed. The Petition for Instructions was continued to March 17, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 in hopes that 1) the Parties will talk to each other and narrow down what the issues are before the Court 2) talk about whether mediation makes sense and 3) give the beneficial Parties the opportunity to file a petition to seek specific relief or wait for the Trustee to present her position to the Court. The petitions if any, are be given a hearing date of March 17, 2014.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)

Petition For Additional Attorneys Fees Incured After Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-07-2007 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearnces: Michael Melchin for petitioner Rose Curreri. Steve Weinkauff for the removed Trustee.

Mr. Melchin submitted a copy of the supplemental declaration for fees upto today. Mr. Weinkauf stated that the Memorandum of Points and Authorities was not filed with the petition and objected to it on procedural and subjective grounds. Mr. Melchin referred to Code 685.040. Mr. Weinkauf objected to the supplemenatal declaration. The Court stated that it was submitted late amd that it would be looked at. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Helen Sorep-Matley CSR 225. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Bonner.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Steven Weinkauf appearing for petitioner. Court has just received the reconciliation of assets. It did not reconciled the deposit from Linda Machi for her share of the insurance premium of $2,038.00 and interest of $2.16 and it was not verified. Court wanted an amended reconciliation. On the attorney's fees issue. Mr. Weinkauf requested for Statement of Decision. Court stated that the request is untimely and Motion is untimely. Oral argument presented by Mr. Weinkauf. Court will accept a brief from counsel to address the merit of the motion by 8/7. Matter will be sumbitted on 8/7.
Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: A. Aguerre.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Mechin (Counsel for Rose Curreri, Petitioner); Steven Weinkauf (Counsel for Linda Machi and Jennie Ghiglieri, Trustees).

After some review of the status of the matter (including the completed preparation of the requested accounting and the adequacy of the existing rental income), the Court continued the matter to January 26, 2007 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for evidentiary hearing.
